misconceptionSummary: Students combine unlike terms or change the variable part when simplifying.

steps.narration: To collect like terms, first look for terms with the same variable part.
Three x and five x are like terms because they both have the same variable part, x. The minus two is a constant term.

lines.text: Keep the x, add the coefficients

steps.narration: Collect the like terms by adding the coefficients. Three x plus five x equals eight x.
Now rewrite the expression in simplified form. The like terms combine to give eight x minus two.

visuals.label: Collect like terms by combining coefficients only

steps.narration: When collecting like terms, the variable part stays the same and only the coefficients change.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: combining 8x and 2 into 10x or 8x2. Constants and x terms are not like terms, so they cannot be collected together.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Only combine terms that match exactly in their variable part. X terms combine with x terms, constants combine with constants.
